entity.summary: Kyrgyzstan-registered, Russia-linked centralized exchange identified by U.S. authorities as a Garantex successor and sanctioned in 2025, then suspended operations in April 2026 after a cyberattack reportedly stole about 1 billion rubles in assets.
entity.notes: Grinex remains inactive rather than dead because the April 2026 announcement confirmed an operational suspension but not permanent liquidation or closure. The Garantex-successor relationship is strongly supported by U.S. Treasury findings but is retained in narrative form until HEI lineage policy explicitly resolves the predecessor relationship.

expected.summary: Kyrgyzstan-registered, Russia-linked centralized exchange widely described as connected to sanctioned Russian crypto infrastructure. In April 2026 it suspended operations after a cyberattack reportedly stole roughly 1 billion rubles, or about USD 13-15 million.
expected.notes: Modeled as inactive rather than dead: Reuters and security research report that Grinex suspended operations after the April 2026 attack, but there is not yet a final shutdown, liquidation, or closure marker. The Garantex-successor / sanctions-evasion context should remain in notes and evidence rather than being used as a standalone death_reason.

changes.summary: Kyrgyzstan-registered, Russia-linked centralized exchange identified by U.S. authorities as a Garantex successor and sanctioned in 2025, then suspended operations in April 2026 after a cyberattack reportedly stole about 1 billion rubles in assets.
changes.notes: Grinex remains inactive rather than dead because the April 2026 announcement confirmed an operational suspension but not permanent liquidation or closure. The Garantex-successor relationship is strongly supported by U.S. Treasury findings but is retained in narrative form until HEI lineage policy explicitly resolves the predecessor relationship.

events.title: U.S. Treasury sanctioned Grinex
events.description: The U.S. Treasury designated Grinex, stating that Garantex employees created it to continue services, transfer customer deposits, and evade sanctions after law-enforcement disruption.
events.notes: The designation establishes strong regulatory and lineage context but did not itself prove immediate platform closure.

events.title: Grinex suspended operations after cyberattack
events.description: Grinex said it suspended operations after a cyberattack stole assets worth approximately 1 billion rubles, reported at about USD 13.1 million.
events.notes: The exchange's attribution claims were not independently verified; HEI records only the confirmed suspension and reported loss.

evidence.title: Treasury Sanctions Cryptocurrency Exchange and Network Enabling Sanctions Evasion and Cyber Criminals
evidence.notes: Official designation describing Grinex as created by Garantex employees and used to continue Garantex-linked services.

evidence.title: Russia-linked Grinex crypto exchange suspends operations after cyber attack
evidence.notes: Independent report of Grinex's operational suspension and reported theft amount after the cyberattack.

evidence.title: Archived Grinex exchange domain
evidence.notes: Archive index preserving the exchange-domain history while current operating status remains unresolved.
